2026-05-18 Meeting Notes

May 19  #914  
Christian Herber
  • All Messages By This Member

Hi all,

 

Minutes from yesterday:


Quick Recap

Christian Herber presented a comprehensive update on the development of a low-latency, vectored, priority-based preemptive interrupt scheme for RISC-V, covering both fundamental extensions and microcontroller fast interrupt extensions. He shared a detailed slide deck outlining the specifications, including hardware vectoring, nested preemption support, and interrupt jump table extensions, while addressing feedback from previous meetings and making significant updates to the specification. The discussion included technical details about stack pointer swapping, preemption models, and interrupt vectoring mechanisms, with Christian explaining his rationale for certain design choices and addressing questions from Paul, Allen, and Alexey about implementation complexity and comparison with alternative approaches. The conversation ended with Christian requesting feedback on how to proceed with the specification and integration into the RISC-V manual, while noting concerns about the ratification plan and testing infrastructure that still need to be addressed.

Next Steps

 

  • Allen: Redraw and improve the preemption diagram slides for better clarity and readability, and coordinate with Christian for the source material.
  • Christian: Share the presentation slides on the mailing list and at the RISC-V Summit.
  • Paul: Open source the test suite and compliance materials for the ACLIC model (Whisper) this week, and update it with the latest specification changes from Christian's pull request.
  • Christian: Review the alternative specification/approach provided by Alexey and provide feedback.
  • All task group members: Provide input and feedback on the direction and features of the specification, particularly regarding the choice between different implementation approaches and their costs.
  • Christian: Write down and propose to the ARC (or relevant group) how the new interrupt extensions should be integrated into the RISC-V manuals (e.g., AIA manual, main ISA manual).
  • All task group members: Discuss and decide on a ratification plan, including SAIL support, testing, and certification requirements for the new interrupt extensions.

 

Summary

 

 

Policy and Compliance Updates Meeting:

Christian began the meeting by covering three key policies: community guidelines, antitrust compliance, and export controls. He then discussed attendance and asked Dan to update his affiliation display. Christian mentioned sending out an email earlier about a slide deck created for the RISC-V Summit and explained that he made a larger update due to small pull requests not receiving quick review input. The conversation ended with Christian starting to discuss the charter.

 

Interrupt Extensions Overview Presentation:

Christian presented a detailed overview of extensions for developing a low-latency, vectored, priority-based preemptive interrupt scheme, focusing on maximizing alignment with AIA specifications. He outlined a pyramid-shaped stack of extensions, including foundational components like indirect CSR interface, hardware vectoring, and nested preemption support, along with microcontroller fast interrupt extensions designed to reduce interrupt latency. Christian also introduced changes to the specification, such as renaming the interrupt vector table extension to "interrupt jump table" and adding features like a top signed interrupt register and programmable entry size for vector tables.

 

Table Access Implementation Details:

Christian discussed the implementation details of table access, explaining his decision to use read instead of fetch due to software dispatcher requirements and PMP settings. He described changes to the stack pointer handling, moving the conditional stack pointer swap before save/after restore operations to ensure M-Mode uses its own stack pointer and maintain validity during non-ABI compliant code execution. Christian also outlined the preemption model, simplifying the extension by removing concepts like fast interrupt enable and explaining how threshold management works during interrupt traps.

 

Software Dispatcher Implementation Update:

Christian presented a software dispenser implementation that differs from the previous proposal shown in past meetings. He explained the technical details of interrupt handling patches, including register saving, mistatus management, and the use of mcause to store interrupt identity in a signed format. Paul inquired about making the slides available, to which Christian confirmed they would be shared on the mailing list and presented at the RISC-V Summit.

 

Interrupt Handling Implementation Discussion:

Christian and Allen discussed the implementation of interrupt handling, focusing on load address expenses and system configurations for RV32 and RV64. Christian explained the priority system for major and external interrupts, clarifying that AIA assigns numeric values for priority levels. Allen requested a redraw of the diagram to improve its clarity, and Christian agreed to send a copy of the presentation for this purpose.

 

Interrupt Handling Specification Discussion:

Christian presented a specification for interrupt handling extensions including preemption and vectoring mechanisms. The discussion focused on comparing different implementation approaches, with Alexey questioning the complexity and cost differences between Christian's proposal and an alternative approach presented earlier. The group identified the need for formalization of pending bit clearing and idempotent save areas to maintain precise exceptions. Key open questions remained about ratification plans, testing infrastructure, and how to integrate the specifications into the manual, with Paul mentioning ongoing work on test suite development and interrupt generation capabilities.



I will send the slides over after receiving the update from Allen.


Regards,

 

Christian

 

  • previous page
  • #914
  • next page

RISC-VењЂйАЯдЄ≠жЦ≠жКАжЬѓдЉЪиЃЃзЇ™и¶БпЉЪChristian HerberдїЛзїНдљОеїґињЯгАБеРСйЗПеМЦгАБеЯЇдЇОдЉШеЕИзЇІзЪДжКҐеН†еЉПдЄ≠жЦ≠жЦєж°ИињЫе±ХпЉМеМЕжЛђз°ђдїґеРСйЗПеМЦгАБеµМе•ЧжКҐеН†жФѓжМБеТМдЄ≠жЦ≠иЈ≥иљђи°®жЙ©е±ХгАВдЉЪиЃЃз°ЃеЃЪдЇЖжФєињЫз§ЇжДПеЫЊгАБеЉАжЇРжµЛиѓХе•ЧдїґгАБиІДиМГеЃ°жЯ•еТМйЫЖжИРеИ∞жЙЛеЖМзЪДеРОзї≠и°МеК®пЉМиЃ®иЃЇдЇЖеЃЮзО∞е§НжЭВжАІеТМжИРжЬђпЉМеЕ≥ж≥®иЃ§иѓБиЃ°еИТеТМжµЛиѓХеЯЇз°АиЃЊжЦљйЬАж±ВгАВ
